metadata.seed: I am fighting with my parents because I am 17 and have just began my first relationship and my parents have grounded me after finding out that I am dating before the age of 18

patient.query: I am fighting with my parents because I am 17 and have just began my first relationship and my parents have grounded me after finding out that I am dating before the age of 18

automatic_thoughts: they don't understand me
I'm being treated unfairly
I should be allowed to date

patient.query: I've just been feeling really trapped lately. It seems like no matter what I do, my parents just don’t understand me. It makes me feel so frustrated and, honestly, kind of alone. I want to make them proud, but I also want to be myself, you know?

I want to make them proud
I want to be myself

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. I mean, it’s like I’m constantly walking on eggshells around them, trying to say what they want to hear. But then, when I do express how I really feel, it feels like they just brush it off. It makes me question if I’m even worth listening to.

automatic_thoughts: trying to say what they want to hear
questioning my worth

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: It’s like a punch to the gut, honestly. I just start to think that maybe my opinions don’t matter at all. I feel small, like I’m not important enough to deserve their attention. Sometimes, I just want to shout at them to really see me, but I worry that would only make things worse.

I’m not important enough to deserve their attention

patient.query: I guess it means feeling like I don’t have to prove anything to anyone. Just being comfortable in my own skin and knowing that my feelings and choices are valid. Sometimes, I get this glimpse of what that feels like, but then it slips away, and I’m left feeling... well, not enough.

My feelings and choices are valid
I’m not enough
